full_prompt: Design one representative mild-steel penstock pipe segment + concrete saddle support for a 10 kW village-scale micro-hydropower scheme per Practical Action's Micro-hydro Design Manual and Technical Brief series. The scheme has a gross head of 60 m, design flow 0.05 m3/s, and a penstock diameter of 200 mm OD mild-steel pipe (spiral-welded, wall thickness 4 mm, grade ASTM A53 Gr B, yield 240 MPa) with a single inline saddle support. Design internal pressure is the static head 60 m = 588 kPa plus a 30 percent surge/water-hammer allowance per Practical Action guidance giving 765 kPa design pressure. The penstock segment under evaluation is 6.0 m long between two saddle supports, with the governing saddle being a mass-concrete block 600 mm x 600 mm x 500 mm embedded in the slope, bearing the pipe with a curved steel cradle lined with rubber. The saddle must resist: (a) vertical dead + water weight of the 6 m pipe segment, approximately m_pipe = rho_steel * V_steel + rho_water * V_water = 7850 * pi * (0.2^2-0.192^2)/4 * 6 + 1000 * pi * 0.192^2/4 * 6 = 116 + 174 = 290 kg, giving 2850 N per saddle, (b) horizontal friction force along pipe axis from thermal expansion = mu_friction * normal = 0.3 * 2850 = 855 N, (c) at bends the thrust from internal pressure (this saddle is nominally at a straight run; bend thrust handled by separate thrust block out of scope). For ropeway ancillary: a single 6x19 galvanized steel carrier cable with safety factor >= 5 per Practical Action ropeway brief is referenced but kept as an out-of-scope secondary artifact. Deliverable is a CAD assembly of one 6 m penstock pipe segment + one saddle support (concrete block + steel cradle + rubber liner) suitable for linear static structural FEA under (a) 765 kPa internal pressure (hoop + longitudinal), (b) vertical saddle reaction 2850 N with realistic bearing footprint, (c) axial friction 855 N. Vibration, thermal, fluid CFD, radiation analyses NOT required. Water hammer is handled as the static 30 percent surge factor; detailed transient fluid simulation is optional.
short_prompt:
Design a 6 m mild-steel penstock pipe segment + concrete saddle support for a 10 kW village-scale micro-hydropower scheme (gross head 60 m, design flow 0.05 m^3/s), per Practical Action's Micro-hydro Design Manual and Technical Brief series. Internal design pressure uses the 30% surge allowance over the 60 m static head. Pipe is ASTM A53 Gr B spiral-welded (yield 240 MPa); saddle is mass-concrete (fc >= 20 MPa) with a rubber-lined steel cradle founded on rock/soil. Allowable pipe hoop stress 0.5 x fy = 120 MPa; saddle-sliding SF 1.5; ground-bearing allowable 200 kPa.

Envelope: 200 mm OD x 4 mm wall penstock pipe, 6.0 m between saddles; saddle concrete block 600 x 600 x 500 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 internal pressure with surge: 765 kPa (60 m head x 1.3 surge factor) inside the pipe.
- LC2 saddle vertical reaction: 2850 N (dead weight of a 6 m water-filled pipe segment) at the saddle bearing.
- LC3 thermal-expansion friction: 855 N axial (0.3 x 2850 N) at the saddle interface.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Optional 1D method-of-characteristics transient hydraulic simulation can be run to refine the 30% surge factor; not a CAE FEA.

prompt.title: Micro-hydro penstock + saddle for 10 kW village scheme
prompt.brief: Design a 6 m segment of 200 mm OD mild-steel penstock + concrete saddle for 60 m head and 765 kPa design pressure per Practical Action.

penstock.grade: ASTM A53 Gr B mild steel

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: gross head + 30% surge per Practical Action
typical soil allowable bearing for compacted fill / weathered rock
Practical Action ropeway brief (cross-referenced, not analyzed in this sample)
pass_fail_criteria.note: Documented for traceability; ropeway is a separate artifact.

analysis_types: linear static internal pressure + saddle reaction + friction

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Thermal expansion captured as friction LC3 only.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Surge handled as 30% static; transient optional.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: External-pressure buckling not governing at 60 m head; long-span pipe buckling handled by saddle spacing.

eval_coverage: pressure-pipe structural FEA with surge factor
